game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **A Dark Room:** Players interact with a minimalist text-based interface, tapping on highlighted words or buttons to perform actions and manage resources. The core loop begins with "light fire" and "collect wood," with wood consumed to maintain the fire, which in turn attracts "wanderers" and unlocks new building options. Building structures like "huts" (e.g., costing 100 wood) expands the village, automating resource generation (e.g., "wood +1/second") and unlocking new actions such as "hunting" or "trapping." Progression is driven by narrative discovery and resource accumulation, eventually leading to the ability to embark on "expeditions" to explore a map, where players manage health points (e.g., 100 HP) during turn-based combat encounters and gather rare materials. The game concludes upon reaching a specific narrative endpoint, with no explicit loss condition in the early stages beyond resource depletion halting progress.
game_info.description: You wake in a cold, dark room with only embers remaining. Your survival depends on gathering wood to keep the fire alive. As warmth returns, strangers appear and your settlement grows. Build structures to automate resource gathering, unlock hunting and trapping, then venture into dangerous expeditions to uncover the truth. Reach the ancient city to complete your journey.
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Navigate menu options and expedition map
Space: Select/confirm action or move forward in expedition
Shift: Cancel/go back in menus
Z: Quick-select first available action
Enter: Start game from title screen
Esc: Pause/unpause game
R: Return to start screen

TEST_1.test_description: Basic functionality test that validates core game mechanics including fire lighting, wood collection, and basic resource management. Tests that actions can be selected and executed, and that resources update correctly.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Repeatedly light the fire and collect wood in alternating fashion to maintain the fire temperature. Verify that wood is consumed when lighting fire and gained when collecting. Monitor fire temperature changes over time.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The test is successful if the player can maintain the fire above freezing, accumulate at least 50 wood, and the fire attracts at least one wanderer. No errors should occur during basic interactions.

TEST_2.test_description: Comprehensive win condition test that validates the complete game progression from starting fire to reaching the ancient city. Tests village building, population growth, expedition mechanics, and narrative completion.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Optimize resource gathering by building huts and workshops early. Use automated wood generation to fund expeditions. Navigate expeditions carefully, managing health and supplies. Reach each location milestone (forest, ruins, mountains, desert, city) while maintaining sufficient resources.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Successfully reach the Ancient City location in expedition mode, triggering the GAME_OVER_WIN state. The entire progression chain should work: fire → village → expeditions → ancient city. Player should survive all encounters and complete the narrative arc.

TEST_3.test_description: Test village expansion and automated resource generation. Validates building mechanics, population management, and resource automation systems work correctly over extended gameplay.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Successfully build at least 5 huts, reach population of 10+ villagers, and verify automated wood generation produces resources without player input. Village should be self-sustaining.
TEST_3.description: Test village expansion and automated resource generation. Validates building mechanics, population management, and resource automation systems work correctly over extended gameplay.
TEST_3.expected: Successfully build at least 5 huts, reach population of 10+ villagers, and verify automated wood generation produces resources without player input. Village should be self-sustaining.

TEST_4.test_description: Test hunting and trapping mechanics. Validates that these actions become available, consume appropriate resources, and produce food/fur consistently.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Once village has sufficient population, unlock and repeatedly use hunting and trapping actions. Verify resource costs are deducted and rewards are granted appropriately.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Successfully hunt and trap multiple times, accumulating food and fur resources. Actions should be gated appropriately by population and available resources.
TEST_4.description: Test hunting and trapping mechanics. Validates that these actions become available, consume appropriate resources, and produce food/fur consistently.

TEST_5.test_description: Test expedition combat and exploration mechanics. Validates turn-based combat, health management, supply consumption, and map navigation.
TEST_5.strategy_description: Embark on expedition with adequate supplies. Engage in combat encounters, testing attack and flee options. Navigate through different terrain types and manage supplies/health.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: Successfully complete at least one expedition encounter through combat. Health should decrease when taking damage, supplies should deplete during travel, and player should be able to flee or fight.
TEST_5.description: Test expedition combat and exploration mechanics. Validates turn-based combat, health management, supply consumption, and map navigation.
